description: Attack 3 from the PHYS-46 program. Uses the subtraction results from experiment_remainder_elliptic_v0 to extract topology-specific elliptic moduli, check consistency within each topology, and run PSLQ with the combined zeta + elliptic basis. If the three integrals within each topology yield the same modulus k and PSLQ finds relations, the six Laporta constants reduce to 2-4 elliptic periods at identified moduli — the first closed forms for four-loop master integrals.
thesis: Each Laporta integral decomposes as C_i = n_i * zeta(k_i) + r_i * f(K(k_topo), E(k_topo), pi) where k_topo is a topology-specific modulus shared by all three integrals within each topology. The consistency of k within each topology is the critical gate.
formula: C_i = n_i * zeta(m) + (p/q) * K(k)^a * E(k)^b * pi^c for topology-specific k
